learningGoal: How to spot hidden words that split 3+1 across word boundaries — it's like finding a secret message!
The trick of checking the last 3 letters of one word joining the first 1 of the next

screens.title: Can you spot the 3+1 split?
screens.body:
Daisy is looking for a 3+1 split across a word boundary.

In a **3+1 split**, the last **3 letters** of one word join the **first 1 letter** of the next to make a hidden word.

Look for: **a 4-letter word meaning 'belonging to me'**

**"The vitamin extra helped her feel better."**

Focus on the words: **vitamin** and **extra**

bodyParts.content:
Daisy is looking for a 3+1 split across a word boundary.

In a **3+1 split**, the last **3 letters** of one word join the **first 1 letter** of the next to make a hidden word.

Look for: **a 4-letter word meaning 'belonging to me'**

**"The vitamin extra helped her feel better."**

Focus on the words: **vitamin** and **extra**

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word
In "vitamin extra":

screens.title: The 3+1 Split — sneaky but findable!
screens.body: Look at **"vitamin extra"** — the clue says: a 4-letter word meaning 'belonging to me'. Grab the last **3 letters** from one word and the **first letter** of the next. Slide your window across and watch the word appear!

bodyParts.content: Look at **"vitamin extra"** — the clue says: a 4-letter word meaning 'belonging to me'. Grab the last **3 letters** from one word and the **first letter** of the next. Slide your window across and watch the word appear!

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word

steps.why: The hidden word spans these two words
Split type: 3+1
The word MINE is hidden across 'vitaMIN' and 'Extra' — MIN(3) + E(1) = MINE, meaning 'belonging to me'. ✓

interaction.correctFeedback: Perfect! Read the clue, find the boundary, then check the 3+1 split. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — start with the clue, then look at word boundaries.

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
Find the hidden word using a 3+1 split.

**"Aladdin escaped through the window."**

Clue: a 4-letter word meaning 'to eat a meal'

bodyParts.content:
Find the hidden word using a 3+1 split.

**"Aladdin escaped through the window."**

Clue: a 4-letter word meaning 'to eat a meal'

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word
Slide across "Aladdin escaped":

interaction.question: Which word is hidden in the sentence?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! The word DINE is hidden across 'aladDIN' and 'Escaped' — DIN(3) + E(1) = DINE, meaning 'to eat a meal'. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "DINE". The word DINE is hidden across 'aladDIN' and 'Escaped' — DIN(3) + E(1) = DINE, meaning 'to eat a meal'. ✓

screens.title: 3+1 Split — you've cracked it!
screens.body: Nice one! Here's your recipe for 3+1 splits:

steps.text: 1. Look at the LAST 3 letters of each word
steps.why: These form the start of the hidden word

steps.text: 2. Add the FIRST letter of the next word
steps.why: This completes the 4-letter hidden word

steps.text: 3. Does it spell a real word matching the clue?
steps.why: electRIC + Engine = RICE ✓

learningGoal: Why 3+1 splits are the sneakiest ones to miss — and how to catch them
Why only checking 2+2 splits will cost you marks

screens.title: Why is the 3+1 split easy to miss?
screens.body:
Evie is reviewing a tricky hidden word question she got wrong.

A friend picked **"FUND"** — thought the answer had to use 2 letters from each word.

But the real answer is hiding in a sneaky **3+1 split** that's easy to miss. Can you spot it?

bodyParts.content:
Evie is reviewing a tricky hidden word question she got wrong.

A friend picked **"FUND"** — thought the answer had to use 2 letters from each word.

But the real answer is hiding in a sneaky **3+1 split** that's easy to miss. Can you spot it?

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word
"FUND" is wrong — can you find the real answer?

screens.title: The 3+1 — don't let it slip past you!
screens.body:
Only checking 2+2 splits means you miss 3+1 combinations like FIN+E = FINE

The golden rule: always try **all** split types — 3+1, 2+2, AND 1+3. Miss one and you might miss the answer!

bodyParts.content:
Only checking 2+2 splits means you miss 3+1 combinations like FIN+E = FINE

The golden rule: always try **all** split types — 3+1, 2+2, AND 1+3. Miss one and you might miss the answer!

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word

steps.why: Only checking 2+2 splits means you miss 3+1 combinations like FIN+E = FINE
Split: 3+1
The word FINE is hidden across 'muFFIN' and 'Ended' — FIN(3) + E(1) = FINE. Always try ALL split types, not just 2+2. ✓

screens.title: Find the 3+1 hidden word!
screens.body:
**"The baton entered the relay race."**

Clue: a 4-letter word meaning 'a musical sound'

bodyParts.content:
**"The baton entered the relay race."**

Clue: a 4-letter word meaning 'a musical sound'

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word
Slide across "baton entered":

interaction.question: Which word is hidden in the sentence?
interaction.correctFeedback: Well done! The word TONE is hidden across 'baTON' and 'Entered' — TON(3) + E(1) = TONE, a musical sound. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "TONE". The word TONE is hidden across 'baTON' and 'Entered' — TON(3) + E(1) = TONE, a musical sound. ✓

screens.title: You won't miss the 3+1 again!
screens.body: Now you know the sneakiest split — here's how to always catch it:

steps.text: 3+1 means: 3 letters from end + 1 letter from start
steps.why: The bigger chunk hides inside the first word

steps.text: Always try ALL split types on EVERY boundary
steps.why: Don't just check 2+2 — try 3+1 and 1+3 too
3 from 'electric' + 1 from 'engine' ✓

learningGoal: How to spot the most common hidden word split — the 2+2 — which shows up loads in the exam
The knack of grabbing the last 2 letters of one word and joining them to the first 2 of the next

screens.title: Can you spot the 2+2 split?
screens.body:
Daisy is looking for 2+2 splits in her VR practice.

A **2+2 split** is the most common: the last **2 letters** of one word join the **first 2 letters** of the next.

Look for: **a 4-letter word meaning 'not closed'**

**"Please stop engines when parking the bus."**

Focus on the words: **stop** and **engines**

bodyParts.content:
Daisy is looking for 2+2 splits in her VR practice.

A **2+2 split** is the most common: the last **2 letters** of one word join the **first 2 letters** of the next.

Look for: **a 4-letter word meaning 'not closed'**

**"Please stop engines when parking the bus."**

Focus on the words: **stop** and **engines**

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word
In "stop engines":

screens.title: The 2+2 Split — the most common one!
screens.body: Look at **"stop engines"** — the clue says: a 4-letter word meaning 'not closed'. Grab the last **2 letters** from one word and the **first 2 letters** of the next. This is the most common split type, so mastering it gives you a real edge!

bodyParts.content: Look at **"stop engines"** — the clue says: a 4-letter word meaning 'not closed'. Grab the last **2 letters** from one word and the **first 2 letters** of the next. This is the most common split type, so mastering it gives you a real edge!

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word

steps.why: The hidden word spans these two words
Split type: 2+2
The word OPEN is hidden across 'stOP' and 'ENgines' — OP(2) + EN(2) = OPEN, meaning 'not closed'. ✓

interaction.sentence: The most common split type for hidden words is the ____ split
interaction.correctFeedback: Yes! The 2+2 split (2 letters from each word) is the most common type. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — the 2+2 split is the most common type of hidden word split!

interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "LAME". The word LAME is hidden across 'vilLA' and 'MEasured' — LA(2) + ME(2) = LAME, meaning 'unable to walk properly'. ✓

screens.title: 2+2 Split — nailed it!
screens.body: Brilliant! You've learned the most common split type. Here's your recipe:

steps.text: 1. Look at the LAST 2 letters of each word
steps.why: These form the start of the hidden word

steps.text: 2. Add the FIRST 2 letters of the next word
steps.why: This completes the 4-letter hidden word

steps.text: 3. Check: real word matching the clue?
steps.why: stOP + ENgines = OPEN ✓

learningGoal: How to catch the trickiest split — the 1+3 — where just ONE letter comes from the first word
Why this split fools so many people, and how you can be the one who spots it

screens.title: Can you spot the 1+3 split?
screens.body:
Grace is searching for 1+3 splits in a sentence.

In a **1+3 split**, just the **last letter** of one word joins the **first 3 letters** of the next.

Look for: **a 4-letter word meaning 'a magic stick'**

**"He finally saw Andrew leave the building."**

Focus on the words: **saw** and **Andrew**

bodyParts.content:
Grace is searching for 1+3 splits in a sentence.

In a **1+3 split**, just the **last letter** of one word joins the **first 3 letters** of the next.

Look for: **a 4-letter word meaning 'a magic stick'**

**"He finally saw Andrew leave the building."**

Focus on the words: **saw** and **Andrew**

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word
In "saw Andrew":

screens.title: The 1+3 Split — super sneaky!
screens.body: Look at **"saw Andrew"** — the clue says: a 4-letter word meaning 'a magic stick'. This time, just the **last letter** from one word joins the **first 3 letters** of the next. Only 1 letter from the first word — that's what makes it so sneaky!

bodyParts.content: Look at **"saw Andrew"** — the clue says: a 4-letter word meaning 'a magic stick'. This time, just the **last letter** from one word joins the **first 3 letters** of the next. Only 1 letter from the first word — that's what makes it so sneaky!

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word

steps.why: The hidden word spans these two words
Split type: 1+3
The word WAND is hidden across 'saW' and 'ANDrew' — W(1) + AND(3) = WAND, a magic stick. ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
Find the hidden word using a 1+3 split.

**"The horse astounded everyone at the show."**

Clue: a 4-letter word meaning 'a compass direction'

bodyParts.content:
Find the hidden word using a 1+3 split.

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word
Slide across "horse astounded":

interaction.question: Which word is hidden in the sentence?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! The word EAST is hidden across 'horsE' and 'ASTounded' — E(1) + AST(3) = EAST, a compass direction. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "EAST". The word EAST is hidden across 'horsE' and 'ASTounded' — E(1) + AST(3) = EAST, a compass direction. ✓

screens.title: 1+3 Split — now you can catch it!
screens.body: Well done — you've learned the sneakiest split! Here's your recipe:

steps.text: 1. Look at the LAST letter of each word
steps.why: Just one letter from the first word!

steps.text: 2. Add the FIRST 3 letters of the next word
steps.why: The bulk of the hidden word comes from word two

steps.text: 3. Check: real word matching the clue?
steps.why: saW + ANDrew = WAND ✓

learningGoal: The surprise twist: sometimes the hidden word is hiding INSIDE a single word!
Why checking inside longer words as well as across boundaries catches answers others miss

screens.title: A word hiding INSIDE another word?
screens.body:
Daisy is surprised to find a word hiding inside another word.

Sometimes the hidden word doesn't span two words at all — it sits entirely **inside** a single word!

Look for: **a 4-letter word meaning 'a narrow road'**

**"The planet orbited the distant star."**

Focus on the words: **planet** and **(within)**

screens.title: Hidden Inside One Word — surprise!
screens.body: The clue says: a 4-letter word meaning 'a narrow road'. Here's something cool — not every hidden word crosses a boundary! Some hide entirely **within** a longer word like **"planet"**. Check the beginning, middle AND end of each word. You'd be amazed what's hiding in there!

steps.why: The hidden word is entirely within this word
No boundary crossing needed!
The word LANE is hidden entirely inside 'pLANEt' — L-A-N-E. No boundary needed! ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
Find the hidden word — it's inside a single word this time!

**"The planet orbited the distant star."**

Clue: a 4-letter word meaning 'a narrow road'

interaction.question: Which word is hidden in the sentence?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! The word LANE is hidden entirely inside 'pLANEt' — L-A-N-E. No boundary needed! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "LANE". The word LANE is hidden entirely inside 'pLANEt' — L-A-N-E. No boundary needed! ✓

screens.title: Inside words — your extra trick!
screens.body: Now you've got an extra trick up your sleeve — hidden words don't always cross boundaries:

steps.text: 1. Check across word boundaries (the sliding window)
steps.why: Try 3+1, 2+2, and 1+3 splits

steps.text: 2. ALSO check inside each individual word
steps.why: Longer words can contain shorter ones
The word hides inside a single word ✓

learningGoal: A time-saving trick: read the clue FIRST so you already know what you're hunting for
Why this one simple habit can make you twice as fast at hidden word questions

screens.title: Read the clue FIRST!
screens.body:
Daisy is learning to read the clue before scanning the sentence.

Before sliding across boundaries, **read the clue**. If you already know what word you're looking for, you can scan much faster!

Clue: **a 4-letter word meaning 'a brave person'**

**"Just before she rode away on her bicycle."**

Focus on the words: **she** and **rode**

bodyParts.content:
Daisy is learning to read the clue before scanning the sentence.

Before sliding across boundaries, **read the clue**. If you already know what word you're looking for, you can scan much faster!

Clue: **a 4-letter word meaning 'a brave person'**

**"Just before she rode away on her bicycle."**

Focus on the words: **she** and **rode**

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word
What word matches the clue?

screens.title: Clue First, Then Scan — so much faster!
screens.body: The clue says: **a 4-letter word meaning 'a brave person'**. First, read the clue and think — what word matches? Once you've got it, scan **"Just before she rode away on her bicycle."** looking for those exact letters. It's like knowing what treasure you're hunting before you start digging!

bodyParts.content: The clue says: **a 4-letter word meaning 'a brave person'**. First, read the clue and think — what word matches? Once you've got it, scan **"Just before she rode away on her bicycle."** looking for those exact letters. It's like knowing what treasure you're hunting before you start digging!

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word

steps.why: Think: what word means this? → HERO

steps.text: Now scan for HERO in the sentence
steps.why: Look for the letters H-E-R-O spanning a boundary
The clue says 'a brave person' — that's HERO! Now find it: sHE + ROde = HERO. Knowing the word makes it faster. ✓

interaction.correctFeedback: Perfect! Clue first, think of the word, then scan. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — always read the clue FIRST, then scan the sentence.

screens.title: Clue First — your speed superpower!
screens.body: This one trick will save you loads of time in the exam. You've got this!

steps.text: 1. Read the clue — what word matches the definition?
steps.why: If you can guess the word, you're halfway there

steps.text: 2. Scan for those SPECIFIC letters in the sentence
steps.why: Much faster than checking every boundary blindly

steps.text: 3. Confirm: do the letters actually span a boundary?
steps.why: Make sure the word is really hidden there ✓

learningGoal: How to hunt down tiny 3-letter hidden words — they're small but mighty tricky!
The two split types for 3-letter words: 2+1 and 1+2 — fewer options but easy to miss

screens.title: Tiny but tricky — 3-letter hidden words!
screens.body:
Daisy is looking for a tiny 3-letter hidden word.

**3-letter words** are short, so they're easy to miss. They split either **2+1** or **1+2** across a boundary.

Look for: **a 3-letter word meaning 'a nocturnal bird'**

**"They had to show Lauren the way to the hall."**

Focus on the words: **show** and **Lauren**

bodyParts.content:
Daisy is looking for a tiny 3-letter hidden word.

**3-letter words** are short, so they're easy to miss. They split either **2+1** or **1+2** across a boundary.

**"They had to show Lauren the way to the hall."**

Focus on the words: **show** and **Lauren**

bodyParts.content: Look at **"show Lauren"** — the clue says: a 3-letter word meaning 'a nocturnal bird'. With only 3 letters, the word splits either **2+1** (two from the first word, one from the next) or **1+2** (one from the first, two from the next).

bodyParts.content: Look at **"show Lauren"** — the clue says: a 3-letter word meaning 'a nocturnal bird'. With only 3 letters, the word splits either **2+1** (two from the first word, one from the next) or **1+2** (one from the first, two from the next).

screens.title: Tiny words, big catches!
screens.body: Great work! 3-letter hidden words are small but you've got the skills to find them:

steps.why: Fewer options, but easy to overlook
Short words hide inside longer ones easily

steps.why: Small words, big catches ✓

learningGoal: How to tackle the big ones — 5-letter hidden words that need more detective work
The four split types to try (4+1, 3+2, 2+3, 1+4) and why the clue is your best friend here

screens.title: Can you find the 5-letter hidden word?
screens.body:
Daisy is tackling a 5-letter hidden word for the first time.

**5-letter words** are harder because they have more split types: 4+1, 3+2, 2+3, or 1+4. You need to try more combinations!

Look for: **a 5-letter word meaning 'something to sit on'**

**"We saw the beach air balloon at the fair."**

Focus on the words: **beach** and **air**

bodyParts.content: Look at **"beach air"** — the clue says: a 5-letter word meaning 'something to sit on'. A 5-letter hidden word can split **4+1, 3+2, 2+3, or 1+4** across a boundary. That's more options, but don't worry — use the clue to narrow your search and you'll find it!

learningGoal: The ultimate twist: sometimes the hidden word reads BACKWARDS — like a mirror message!
Why checking both directions is your secret weapon when you feel stuck

screens.title: What if the word reads BACKWARDS?
screens.body:
Daisy is discovering that hidden words can read backwards.

Some hidden word questions have a twist — the word reads **backwards** through the sentence! If you can't find it forwards, try reversing.

Look for: **a 4-letter word meaning 'a section or piece' — reading backwards!**

**"The extra passengers boarded the bus quickly."**

Focus on the words: **extra** and **passengers**

bodyParts.content: Look at **"extra passengers"** — the clue says: a 4-letter word meaning 'a section or piece' — reading backwards!. Can't find the word going forwards? Try reading the letters **backwards** across the boundary — it's like looking in a mirror!

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word
Try reading backwards across "extra passengers":

screens.title: Reading Backwards — flip it!
screens.body: Look at **"extra passengers"** — the clue says: a 4-letter word meaning 'a section or piece' — reading backwards!. Can't find the word going forwards? Try reading the letters **backwards** across the boundary — it's like looking in a mirror!

bodyParts.content: Look at **"extra passengers"** — the clue says: a 4-letter word meaning 'a section or piece' — reading backwards!. Can't find the word going forwards? Try reading the letters **backwards** across the boundary — it's like looking in a mirror!

props.label: Slide the purple letters across to find the hidden word

steps.why: These letters span the boundary
Reverse the order to reveal the word
The letters T-R-A-P are hidden across 'exTRA' and 'Passengers'. Read backwards: P-A-R-T = PART, meaning 'a section'. ✓

interaction.sentence: If you can't find the hidden word going forwards, try reading the letters ____
interaction.correctFeedback: Yes! Some hidden words read backwards across the boundary. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — try reading the letters BACKWARDS to reveal the hidden word!

interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "PART". The letters T-R-A-P are hidden across 'exTRA' and 'Passengers'. Read backwards: P-A-R-T = PART, meaning 'a section'. ✓

screens.title: Forwards AND backwards — you've got both covered!
screens.body: Now you know to check both directions. That puts you ahead of most people!

steps.text: 1. Try finding the word FORWARDS first
steps.why: Most hidden words read left to right
Some tricky questions reverse the word
Reverse the letters to find the answer ✓

learningGoal: The clever trick exam-setters use: making the hidden word mean something totally different from the sentence!
How to ignore the sentence's topic and focus on what actually matters — the letters

screens.title: The hidden word doesn't match the sentence!
screens.body:
Daisy is noticing the hidden word means something different to the sentence.

Here's the trick: the **hidden word** often means something completely **different** to what the sentence is about. This misdirection is deliberate — it makes the word harder to spot!

Look for: **a 4-letter word meaning 'water falling from the sky'**

**"Pack your camera inside before it gets dark."**

Focus on the words: **camera** and **inside**

screens.title: Don't fall for the red herring!
screens.body:
The sentence is about packing a camera — nothing to do with weather!

Here's the key: don't look for a word that **fits** the sentence — focus on one that matches the **clue**. The sentence is trying to distract you!

screens.title: You're too smart for their tricks!
screens.body: Now you know the secret: hidden word sentences are DESIGNED to mislead — but you won't fall for it!

steps.why: It's there to distract you from the hidden word

steps.why: cameRA + INside = RAIN (nothing to do with cameras!) ✓
